title: plantago coronopus subvar. integrata
intro: Plantago coronopus subvar. integrata, also known as buck's horn or buck's horn plantain, is a type of plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of buck's horn plantain include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for treating conditions such as diarrhea, wounds, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, buck's horn plantain can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves in hot water, or used as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
